req.title: Manager views policy-document
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to view a policy-document through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path view by an authenticated manager (EP – valid partition), (2) unauthenticated access returning 401 (NEG), (3) forbidden access by a non-manager role returning 403 (DT – role decision), (4) a boundary case exercising the maximum-length policy-document title field (BVA), and (5) a state-transition case verifying the audit record is written only after a successful view (STC). Together these cover the stated error pathways (400/401/403), the audit requirement, and the entity-id assertion.

tests.name: test_manager_views_policy_document_success
preconditions: At least one policy-document exists in the system
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_auth_token>
GET /policy-documents/<existing_doc_id> with Authorization header
Assert HTTP 200 and body contains entity id
Query audit log for a record matching the view event
expected: Response body contains the policy-document entity id
Audit record with action=view and actor=manager is written
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"


def test_manager_views_policy_document_success(
    manager_auth_token,   # <fixture>
    existing_policy_doc,  # <fixture> returns {"id": "<uuid>", ...}
    audit_log_client,     # <fixture>
):
    doc_id = existing_policy_doc["id"]
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_auth_token}"}

    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/policy-documents/{doc_id}", headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["id"] == doc_id

    audit_entry = audit_log_client.find_latest(
        resource="policy-document",
        resource_id=doc_id,
        action="view",
    )
    assert audit_entry is not None
    assert audit_entry["actor_role"] == "manager"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_policy_document
actions: Send GET /policy-documents/<existing_doc_id> without any Authorization header
Assert HTTP 401 is returned
Assert no audit record is created for this attempt
expected: Response body contains an authentication error message
No audit record is written for this unauthenticated attempt
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"


def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_policy_document(
    existing_policy_doc,  # <fixture>
    audit_log_client,     # <fixture>
):
    doc_id = existing_policy_doc["id"]

    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/policy-documents/{doc_id}")

    assert resp.status_code == 401
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

    audit_entry = audit_log_client.find_latest(
        resource="policy-document",
        resource_id=doc_id,
        action="view",
    )
    assert audit_entry is None

tests.name: test_non_manager_role_forbidden_from_viewing_policy_document
preconditions: User is authenticated with role:employee (not manager)
actions: Obtain a valid employee (non-manager) auth token via <get_auth_token>
GET /policy-documents/<existing_doc_id> with employee Authorization header
Assert HTTP 403 is returned
Assert no audit record is created for this forbidden attempt
expected: Response body contains a permission/forbidden error
No audit record is written for this forbidden attempt
trace: R-WEB-056 – error pathway: 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"


def test_non_manager_role_forbidden_from_viewing_policy_document(
    employee_auth_token,  # <fixture> – role:employee
    existing_policy_doc,  # <fixture>
    audit_log_client,     # <fixture>
):
    doc_id = existing_policy_doc["id"]
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_auth_token}"}

    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/policy-documents/{doc_id}", headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

    audit_entry = audit_log_client.find_latest(
        resource="policy-document",
        resource_id=doc_id,
        action="view",
    )
    assert audit_entry is None

tests.name: test_manager_views_policy_document_with_max_length_title
preconditions: A policy-document with a title at the maximum allowed length (255 chars) exists
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_auth_token>
Create (or retrieve) a policy-document whose title is exactly 255 characters via <create_policy_doc_fixture>
GET /policy-documents/<doc_id> with Authorization header
Assert HTTP 200 and entity id matches
Assert audit record written
expected: Audit record with action=view is written
trace: R-WEB-056 – mandatory field validation (title boundary at max length)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"
MAX_TITLE_LEN = 255


def test_manager_views_policy_document_with_max_length_title(
    manager_auth_token,       # <fixture>
    create_policy_doc,        # <fixture> factory: create_policy_doc(title=...)
    audit_log_client,         # <fixture>
):
    max_title = "A" * MAX_TITLE_LEN
    doc = create_policy_doc(title=max_title)
    doc_id = doc["id"]
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_auth_token}"}

    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/policy-documents/{doc_id}", headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["id"] == doc_id
    assert len(body["title"]) == MAX_TITLE_LEN

    audit_entry = audit_log_client.find_latest(
        resource="policy-document",
        resource_id=doc_id,
        action="view",
    )
    assert audit_entry is not None

tests.name: test_audit_record_written_only_after_successful_view_state_transition
preconditions: A policy-document exists and has NOT been viewed yet in this test run
actions: Confirm no audit record for view exists before the request
GET /policy-documents/<doc_id> with manager Authorization header
Assert HTTP 200
Confirm exactly one new audit record for view now exists
expected: Pre-condition: audit record count for this doc+action is 0
Post-condition: audit record count for this doc+action is exactly 1
State transition: unviewed -> viewed is reflected in audit log
trace: R-WEB-056 – audit record written on success
R-WEB-056 – state transition: policy-document moves from unviewed to viewed
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"


def test_audit_record_written_only_after_successful_view_state_transition(
    manager_auth_token,       # <fixture>
    fresh_policy_doc,         # <fixture> creates a brand-new doc, never viewed
    audit_log_client,         # <fixture>
):
    doc_id = fresh_policy_doc["id"]
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_auth_token}"}

    pre_count = audit_log_client.count(
        resource="policy-document", resource_id=doc_id, action="view"
    )
    assert pre_count == 0, "Audit records must not exist before the view"

    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/policy-documents/{doc_id}", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    post_count = audit_log_client.count(
        resource="policy-document", resource_id=doc_id, action="view"
    )
    assert post_count == 1, "Exactly one audit record must be created after view"
